provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Declaration of Assets and Liabilities Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Application
Establishment of Directorate
provisions.content:
4. Establishment of Directorate
(1) There is hereby established an Ethics and Integrity Directorate.
(2) The Directorate shall consist of a Director-General and such other officers of the Directorate, as may be necessary for the proper performance of the functions of the Directorate.
(3) The Directorate shall be a public office and accordingly, the provisions of the Public Service Act shall with such modifications as may be necessary, apply to the Director-General and to officers of the Directorate.
(4) Subject to the provisions of this Act, the Directorate shall not, in the performance of its functions, be subject to the direction or control of any other person or authority.

provisions.title: Appointment of Director-General
provisions.content:
5. Appointment of Director-General
(1) There shall be a Director-General who shall be appointed by the President on such terms and conditions as the President may, on the recommendation of the Minister, determine.
(2) A person appointed as a Director-General shall hold office for a five year renewable term or until he or she attains the age of 60 years, whichever is the earlier.
(3) A person holding the office of Director-General may be removed from office for—
( a ) inability to perform the functions of his or her office arising from infirmity of body, mind or any other cause;
( b ) gross misconduct; or
( c ) incompetence.
(4) The provisions of section 113(3), (4) and (5) of the Constitution shall apply with necessary modifications to the removal of a person holding office of Director-General.
(5) The Director-General shall be responsible for the direction and administration of the Directorate.

provisions.title: Points of declarations
provisions.content:
6. Points of declarations
(1) A declaration shall be made—
( a ) to the Minister responsible for Presidential Affairs, Governance and Public Administration, by the Director-General;
( b ) to the Director-General—
(i) by the President,

definitions.definition: a declaration of interest, income, assets and liabilities in accordance with this Act
the Director-General of the Ethics and Integrity Directorate appointed under section 5
the Ethics and Integrity Directorate established under section 4
an authority empowered by an Act of Parliament to investigate or prosecute unlawful practices

assets of any kind, whether corporeal or incorporeal, tangible or intangible, movable or immovable, however acquired, and legal documents or instruments in any form, including electronic or digital, evidencing title to, or interest in such assets
responsibility in accordance with the law to satisfy a debt, duty or obligation quantifiable in monetary value, present and contingent, of a total value of P200 000 or above
assigned to it in the Livestock and Meat Industries Act (Cap. 36:03)
a city council, a town council, a township authority, a district council, a sub-district council, an administrative authority or a landboard established under the Tribal Land Act (Cap. 32:02)
a magistrate as defined under the Magistrates&rsquo
assigned to it under the Proceeds and Instruments of Crime Act (Cap. 08:03)

a voluntary organisation, non-governmental organisation, political party, charitable institution, society, association, company, partnership, club, trust or any other person or organisation where public moneys are used
any office, organisation, establishment or body created by or under any enactment or under powers conferred by any enactment, and includes any company in which Government has equity shares
a public officer as defined under the Public Service Act (Cap. 26:01), and any other person holding a public office under any other enactment
a register established under section 10(3)( i )
the register established and maintained under section 10(3)( h )
the Registrar of the Court of Appeal, the Registrar of the High Court, the Registrar of the Industrial Court, and includes their Deputy Registrars and Assistant Registrars.
